name: Semantic Versioning and Release Workflow
description: Use npm version commands to automate semantic versioning, generate git tags, and create a consistent release workflow for your packages.
trendingReason: Proper versioning is essential for reliable package ecosystems and automated release pipelines

steps.title: Check the current version
steps.description: View the current version of your package.

steps.title: Bump a patch version
steps.description: Increment the patch version for backward-compatible bug fixes.
steps.tip: npm version automatically updates package.json, creates a git commit, and tags it. The %s placeholder is replaced with the new version.

steps.title: Bump a minor version
steps.description: Increment the minor version for backward-compatible new features.

steps.title: Bump a major version
steps.description: Increment the major version for breaking changes.
steps.warning: Major version bumps signal breaking changes. Document all breaking changes in your changelog before releasing.

steps.title: Create a prerelease version
steps.description: Publish beta or RC versions for testing before stable release.
steps.tip: This creates versions like 1.0.1-beta.0, 1.0.1-beta.1. Publish with 'npm publish --tag beta' to avoid affecting the latest tag.

steps.title: Publish with the correct dist-tag
steps.description: Release the new version with the appropriate tag.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# npm Version and Release Workflow
set -euo pipefail

BUMP_TYPE=${1:?"Usage: $0 <patch|minor|major|prerelease> [--preid=beta]"}

echo "=== Current Version ==="
CURRENT=$(npm pkg get version | tr -d '"')
echo "v$CURRENT"

echo ""
echo "=== Pre-Release Checks ==="
npm run lint 2>/dev/null && echo "Lint: passed" || echo "Lint: skipped"
npm test 2>/dev/null && echo "Tests: passed" || echo "Tests: skipped"
npm run build 2>/dev/null && echo "Build: passed" || echo "Build: skipped"

echo ""
echo "=== Bumping Version ==="
if [ "$BUMP_TYPE" = "prerelease" ]; then
  NEW_VERSION=$(npm version prerelease --preid=${2:-beta} -m "chore(release): v%s")
else
  NEW_VERSION=$(npm version "$BUMP_TYPE" -m "chore(release): v%s")
fi
echo "New version: $NEW_VERSION"

echo ""
echo "=== Next Steps ==="
echo "1. Review: git log --oneline -3"
echo "2. Push:   git push origin main --follow-tags"
if [[ "$BUMP_TYPE" == "prerelease" ]]; then
  echo "3. Publish: npm publish --tag beta"
else
  echo "3. Publish: npm publish"
fi

faq.question: What do the semver numbers mean?
faq.answer: In MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH: MAJOR changes indicate breaking API changes, MINOR changes add backward-compatible features, and PATCH changes are backward-compatible bug fixes. For example, going from 2.3.1 to 3.0.0 signals breaking changes.

faq.question: How do I prevent npm version from creating a git tag?
faq.answer: Use --no-git-tag-version to only update package.json without creating a commit or tag: 'npm version patch --no-git-tag-version'. This is useful when you manage tags separately.

faq.question: What are dist-tags and how do they work?
faq.answer: Dist-tags are labels like 'latest', 'beta', 'next' that point to specific versions. When users run 'npm install pkg', they get the 'latest' tag. Use 'npm publish --tag beta' to publish without affecting 'latest', letting users opt in with 'npm install pkg@beta'.
